briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware firm Synapse, highlighting serious risks in the bank-fintech partnership model.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign European payment system in 2026, signalling a major shift away from US-dominated card networks.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is disrupting traditional credit card models with new features, rewards structures, and embedded finance experiences that challenge legacy issuers.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what that means for consumers and incumbent financial institutions.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Bank of Canada says financial system is in good shape, but vulnerabilities have increased](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-financial-stability-9.7215056?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada's latest financial stability review finds the system resilient overall, but warns that rising vulnerabilities could pose risks if economic conditions deteriorate.

- **[Canada slipped into a technical recession on an annualized basis as economic growth stalled in 1st quarter](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/recession-gdp-may-2026-statscan-9.7216352?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Statistics Canada data shows the Canadian economy entered a technical recession in Q1 2026, raising pressure on the Bank of Canada and financial sector stakeholders.

- **[Questrade's Salim Naran doesn't understand why more people aren't using AI for personal finance](https://betakit.com/questrades-salim-naran-doesnt-understand-why-more-people-arent-using-ai-for-personal-finance/)** — *BetaKit* — Questrade's head of product argues that AI-powered personal finance tools are underutilized by Canadians despite their potential to meaningfully improve financial outcomes.

- **[Upset Uber customers charged for monthly memberships they say they never signed up for](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/uber-one-membership-charges-9.7212871?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian Uber users are reporting unauthorized charges for Uber One memberships, raising consumer protection and embedded subscription billing concerns.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* *(flagged for relevance to Canada's open banking journey)* — Canada's federal government signalled a move away from screen scraping toward a secure open banking framework, a milestone in the country's still-evolving consumer-directed finance journey.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canadian startups face a home-market adoption problem, CentML co-founder says](https://betakit.com/canadian-startups-face-a-home-market-adoption-problem-centml-co-founder-says/)** — *BetaKit* — A Canadian AI startup founder highlights the persistent challenge of domestic market adoption, a barrier highly relevant to Atlantic Canadian fintech companies seeking to scale locally before going global.

- **[Arteria AI CEO Shelby Austin is wary of Canada naming national tech champions](https://betakit.com/arteria-ai-ceo-shelby-austin-is-wary-of-canada-naming-national-tech-champions/)** — *BetaKit* — The CEO of Canadian legal-financial AI firm Arteria AI cautions against government picking winners in the tech sector, a debate with direct implications for how Atlantic fintech startups access federal support.

- **[Government considering amendments to Bill C-22 amid backlash from tech, civil liberties groups](https://betakit.com/government-considering-amendments-to-bill-c-22-amid-backlash-from-tech-civil-liberties-groups/)** — *BetaKit* — The Canadian government is weighing changes to Bill C-22 following pushback from technology and civil liberties advocates, a regulatory development worth monitoring for its potential impact on fintech innovation and compliance obligations.
